name: Santuario de Belén to Embalse de Almansa — circular
name_original: Santuario de Belén al Embalse de Almansa — circular
region_intro: Hiking around Almansa offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by mountain ranges, extensive pine forests, and the historic Almansa Reservoir. The region features varied terrain, including limestone formations in the Sierra del Mugrón and wooded pastures with holm oaks. Trails often follow riverbanks, pass by historic mills, and ascend to viewpoints offering expansive vistas. This area provides a range of hiking experiences across its distinct natural features. Best hiking trails around Almansa The most popular hiking route is Pantano de Almansa — circular, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 56 minutes to complete. This route offers views of one of Europe's oldest dams and the surrounding reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sierra de Oliva Circular Trail, a moderate 8.6 miles (13.9 km) path. This trail leads through the Sierra de Oliva, ascending to the Hermitage of Santa Bárbara, which provides panoramic views. Local hikers also love the Rambla de los Molinos Loop, a 7.5 miles (12.0 km) trail leading through riverbank groves and past historic mills, often completed in about 3 hours 11 minutes. Hiking around Almansa is defined by reservoir paths, mountain ascents, and forested ramblas, with options available for various ability levels. The routes in Almansa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 13 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Almansa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Almansa?
A: There are over 45 hiking trails around Almansa, offering diverse experiences for various skill levels. Among these, 17 are classified as easy, 26 as moderate, and 3 as difficult.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Almansa?
A: Yes, Almansa offers several easy hiking trails perfect for beginners and families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1102818963/agua-viva-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Agua Viva Trail&lt;/a&gt; around the Almansa Reservoir is a great option, featuring a unique floating walkway and picturesque views. The reservoir area generally provides accessible circular paths.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Almansa?
A: Yes, many of Almansa's trails are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084207276/sierra-de-oliva-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Oliva Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views from the Hermitage of Santa Bárbara.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Almansa?
A: Almansa's hiking trails traverse diverse natural landscapes. You'll encounter extensive pine forests, particularly Aleppo and black pines, alongside rich Mediterranean scrubland. The region also features the historic Almansa Reservoir, the limestone formations of the Sierra del Mugrón, and wooded pastures with holm oaks. Riverbank groves and historic mills are also common sights.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the hiking trails in Almansa?
A: Yes, Almansa offers several scenic viewpoints. The Hermitage of Santa Bárbara, accessible via the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084207276/sierra-de-oliva-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Oliva Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, provides expansive vistas. The Almansa Reservoir also offers picturesque views, especially from its dam and floating walkway. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4713383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Museum Trail&lt;/a&gt; and other scenic spots in the Natural Monuments around Almansa.

Q: What historical or natural attractions can I explore near the hiking routes?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore several historical and natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5287878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Almansa&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent landmark. The region also features the ruins of the Picabarajas cave house, and historic mills along routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1102863781/rambla-de-los-molinos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rambla de los Molinos Loop&lt;/a&gt;. The Almansa Reservoir itself is one of Europe's oldest dams, built in 1584.

Q: Is Almansa a good destination for nature observation, especially birds and wildlife?
A: Absolutely. Almansa is rich in biodiversity, making it ideal for nature observation. The Almansa Reservoir is an important ecological area supporting diverse avifauna. The Sierra del Mugrón is a quiet, isolated area teeming with wildlife, including wildcats, boars, various birds of prey, and migratory birds like great bustards and wood pigeons.

Q: Are there public recreation areas available for rest stops or picnics during hikes?
A: Yes, Almansa provides several public recreation areas equipped with amenities. Places like Fuente del Escudero, Santuario de Belén, and Olula offer potable water, tables, benches, barbecues, and parking, making them convenient spots for a break or picnic during your outdoor excursions.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Almansa?
A: While hiking is enjoyable year-round, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for exploring Almansa's diverse terrain. Summers can be warm, but the Almansa Reservoir provides opportunities for aquatic activities and refreshing stops. Winters are typically mild.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Almansa?
A: The hiking trails in Almansa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 13 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from reservoir paths to mountain ascents and forested ramblas, and the opportunities to discover historical and natural landmarks.

Q: Are there any trails that follow riverbanks or feature water elements?
A: Yes, the region offers trails that incorporate water elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1102863781/rambla-de-los-molinos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rambla de los Molinos Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads through refreshing riverbank groves and past historic mills, including the Molino de la Torre with its 2 km long canal. The Almansa Reservoir also features trails directly alongside the water.
tips: It is located in a quiet place, surrounded by nature, surrounded by an extensive pine forest. The sanctuary itself is marvelous, with a unique golden baroque altarpiece in the province of Albacete. There are barbecues and drinking fountains.

One of the oldest dams in Europe (the first works began between 1530 and 1538) that stores temporary torrents caused by the rains. A wonderful setting, it even has a jetty and water activities center.
